---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size60[
.center[
Evaluate the Political Terror Scale (PTS) research project
]]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

### Prep for Class
1. ...

<br>

On Monday we evaluated reports by the US State Department and Amnesty International as sources for measuring political violence around the world.

### What were our key takeaways on Monday?

#### - Are these sources "better" than using media reporting? Why or why not?

<br>

Today and Friday we evaluate two research projects that use these sources to create measures of political violence around the world and across time. 



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.center[**For Today**]]

.size40[
**Explore the data and codebook for the [Political Terror Scale (PTS)](http://www.politicalterrorscale.org/Data/Download.html). Make sure you understand the observations and variables enough to play with them in class.**

*Recommended Links (in syllabus):*

1. [Sorting Rows in Excel](https://www.excel-easy.com/data-analysis/sort.html)
2. [Filtering Rows in Excel](https://www.excel-easy.com/data-analysis/filter.html)
3. [Pivot Tables in Excel](https://www.excel-easy.com/data-analysis/pivot-tables.html)
]

???

### Everybody ready for today's work?

#### - Assignment complete?

<br>

Everybody organize yourselves into groups of 3-4, circle the chairs

Take a few minutes to make sure everyone in your group can:

1. Open the data in Excel,

2. Understands how to do the basic data analyses in Excel (sort and filter), and 

3. Is clear on the observations and variables in the data project (use the codebook as needed)



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/03-2-PTS_Logo.png')
background-size: 70%
background-position: top
class: center, middle, slideblue

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

.size60[Compare and contrast the definition of "political terror" to our definition from last week]

???

Groups: Take a few minutes to do this together and then we'll report back.

<br>

### Ok, so, how do these definitions compare?

#### - What are the strengths and weaknesses of this definition?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/03-2-PTS_Logo.png')
background-size: 70%
background-position: top
class: slideblue, center, middle

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

.size60[Discuss the strengths and weaknesses of the data collection methodology (e.g. Sources, Units)]

???

Groups: Take a few minutes to do this together and then we'll report back.

<br>

### Ok, report back.

#### - What are the strengths and weaknesses?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/03_2-PTS_Levels.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

???

### GROUPS: Discuss the strengths and weaknesses of the coding scheme for the pts variable

<br>

### Ok, report back.
#### - What are the strengths and weaknesses?

<br>

Audit Time!

Each of you focus on the scores for the three countries you analyzed on Monday. 

Go back to the reports and see if you would have coded them the same way. 

### Findings? Takeaways?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size70[.center[**Analysis Time!**]]

<br>

.size40[
1) Focus on 2020 (most recent year coded), how much conflict across: PTS_A, PTS_H and PTS_S? 
   + Specific examples?
]

```{r, eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
d <- read_excel("../../Data/PTS/PTS-2021.xlsx", na = "NA")

d2019 <- d |>
  filter(Year == 2020)

d2019 |> count(PTS_A, PTS_H, PTS_S) |> print(n=100)

## Matching on 42%
d2019 |> 
  count(PTS_A, PTS_H, PTS_S) |>
  na.omit() |>
  mutate(
    matching = if_else(PTS_A == PTS_H & PTS_A == PTS_S, 1*n, 0)
  ) |>
  summarize(
    sum(matching) / sum(n)
  )
```


???

*Groups work on slide question, report back and then segue to questions below*

*Some R Code in Rmd file!*

<br>

### Do differences within a country-year tell us more about the use of violence in that state, bias in the sources of the data or problems with the pts measure they have designed?

<br>

### Does this help us understand the value in including all three measures?

#### - Do all three bring greater confidence to our understanding?
#### - Capture different things? 
#### - Add robustness?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size70[.center[**Analysis Time!**]]

<br>

.size40[
2) Repeat using 2014 (oldest year with data in all three vars), how much conflict across: PTS_A, PTS_H and PTS_S? 
   + Specific examples?
]

```{r, eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
d <- read_excel("../../Data/PTS/PTS-2021.xlsx", na = "NA")

d |> count(Year, PTS_H) |> print(n=100)
d |> count(Year, PTS_A) |> print(n=300)
d |> count(Year, PTS_S) |> print(n=300)

d2014 <- d |>
  filter(Year == 2014)

d2014 |> count(PTS_A, PTS_H, PTS_S) |> print(n=100)

## Matching on 55% of observations
d2014 |> 
  count(PTS_A, PTS_H, PTS_S) |>
  na.omit() |>
  mutate(
    matching = if_else(PTS_A == PTS_H & PTS_A == PTS_S, 1*n, 0)
  ) |>
  summarize(
    sum(matching) / sum(n)
  )
```

???

*Groups work on slide question, report back and then segue to questions below*

*Some R Code in Rmd file!*

<br>

### Does this help us answer that question about where the error may lie / how useful it is to keep all three measures?

#### - More disagreement in the past?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size70[.center[**Analysis Time!**]]

<br>

.size40[
3) Focus on 2020 and sort all countries by PTS_S.

  + Any surprises? 

  + Any countries with a score that challenges your intuitions?
]

```{r, eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
d <- read_excel("../../Data/PTS/PTS-2021.xlsx", na = "NA")

d2020 <- d |>
  filter(Year == 2020)

d2020 |>
  arrange(PTS_S) |>
  select(Country, Year, PTS_S) |>
  View()

```

???

*Groups work on slide question, report back and then segue to questions below*

*Some R Code in Rmd file!*

<br>


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size70[.center[**Analysis Time!**]]

<br>

.size40[
4) Focus on the years since 2001 and find us examples of countries that have:

  + Improved the most across the years in the sample, and
  
  + Regressed the most across the years in the sample
]

```{r, eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
d <- read_excel("../../Data/PTS/PTS-2021.xlsx", na = "NA")

d |> count(Year, PTS_H) |> print(n=100)
d |> count(Year, PTS_A) |> print(n=300)
d |> count(Year, PTS_S) |> print(n=300)

d |>
  select(Country, Year, PTS_S) |>
  filter(Year %in% c(2001, 2020)) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Year, values_from = PTS_S) |>
  mutate(
    Change = `2020` - `2001`
  ) |> View()

```

???

*Groups work on slide question, report back and then segue to questions below*

*Some R Code in Rmd file!*

<br>

### Report back and takeaways?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size70[.center[**Analysis Time!**]]

<br>

.size40[
5) Go back to 2020 and focus on the OECD region identifier (not individual countries).

Which regions are the most and least problematic? 

Any Surprises?
]

```{r, eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
d <- read_excel("../../Data/PTS/PTS-2021.xlsx", na = "NA")

d2020 <- d |>
  filter(Year == 2020)

d2020 |>
  group_by(Region) |>
  summarize(
    Mean_A = mean(PTS_A, na.rm=TRUE),
    Mean_H = mean(PTS_H, na.rm=TRUE),
    Mean_S = mean(PTS_S, na.rm=TRUE)
  )

d2020 |>
  group_by(Region) |>
  summarize(
    Median_A = median(PTS_A, na.rm=TRUE),
    Median_H = median(PTS_H, na.rm=TRUE),
    Median_S = median(PTS_S, na.rm=TRUE)
  )
```

???

*Groups work on slide question, report back and then segue to questions below*

<br>

### Bottom line, how useful is the pts for helping us test theories of political violence? Strengths and weaknesses?



---
background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size60[.center[**For Friday**]]

.size50[
**Explore the data and codebook for the [CIRIGHTS Data project](https://www.dropbox.com/sh/t8utmzsvde8m63q/AAAs1_WIJTqXurAE5nvEKWE5a?dl=0).**

Make sure you understand the observations and variables enough to play with them in class.
]

???

For Friday we repeat today's work on the CIRIGHTS data project.

Get ready to work!
